title: Enumeration Deep Dive
learning_objectives: Perform comprehensive service enumeration across multiple hosts
key_topics: Service-specific techniques (HTTP, SMB, FTP, SSH, DNS)
scenario: You have been given access to a lab network with 3 hosts. Your mission: perform thorough enumeration of each host. Discover everything you can about ports, services, versions, OS, shares, and web directories, all without exploitation.

scenario_context.setting: Lab network with 3 diverse target hosts
scenario_context.relevance: Thorough enumeration is what separates a methodical professional from a random scanner. Every service discovered is a potential attack vector.

subtasks.title: Port Discovery
subtasks.description: Scan all 3 hosts using at least 2 scanning techniques. Compare results and document all port states.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Use SYN scan and connect scan. Compare results. Note any differences — they reveal firewall behaviour. Document open, closed, and filtered ports.
subtasks.evidence_required: Scan results from both techniques plus comparison notes

subtasks.title: Service Enumeration
subtasks.description: For every open port, determine exact service and version using banner grabbing and service probes.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Use nmap -sV for version detection. Manually banner-grab with netcat where possible. Note default configurations, outdated versions, and unusual services.
subtasks.evidence_required: Service enumeration table: host, port, service, version

subtasks.title: Web Directory Enumeration
subtasks.description: For any web servers found, perform directory enumeration. Document discovered paths, status codes, and interesting files.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Use Gobuster with a common wordlist. Check robots.txt manually. Note interesting directories: admin panels, backup files, config files. Record status codes.

subtasks.title: Enumeration Summary
subtasks.description: Create a comprehensive report covering all 3 hosts with attack surface analysis.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: For each host: summary of services, potential vulnerabilities (based on versions), interesting findings. Include an attack surface map showing the most promising vectors.
subtasks.evidence_required: Complete enumeration report with attack surface map

reflection_prompt: One of the hosts is running a service you've never seen before on port 8888. Describe your methodology for identifying and researching an unknown service.

quiz.question: Comparing SYN and connect scan results is useful because:
options: Differences reveal firewall rules and filtering behaviour
quiz.explanation: Firewalls may treat SYN and full connect differently. Comparing results reveals filtering rules.

quiz.question: Directory enumeration with Gobuster finds:
options: Web paths and files that aren't linked from the main site
quiz.explanation: Gobuster brute-forces web paths using wordlists, discovering admin panels, backup files, and other unlisted content.

mentor_notes: Completeness is critical. A submission that misses a major service on any host should lose significant points. The attack surface analysis shows strategic thinking.
